description: Neon-soaked neighborhoods layered over centuries of craftsmanship and culinary obsession.

food: Queue early for omakase at Sushi Dai in Toyosu Market.
Warm up with tantanmen at Nakiryu, one of Tokyo's few Michelin ramen shops.
Dessert crawl through the depachika food halls beneath Ginza's grand department stores.
shopping: Wander the back streets of Daikanyama for design-forward boutiques and coffee labs.
Hit the menswear wonderland at BEAMS and United Arrows in Harajuku.
Scout Akihabara's retro game shops for mint-condition classics.
sightseeing: Catch sunset at Roppongi's Mori Tower sky deck.
Take the Chuo Line to Mitaka for the Studio Ghibli Museum.
Spend a quiet morning at Meiji Jingu before the city wakes.

description: Scandinavian minimalism, canal-side hygge, and bicycles somehow everywhere at once.

food: Book far ahead for a long lunch at Geranium or Alchemist.
Grab a smørrebrød board at Café Norden off Strøget.
Sip natural wine and snacks at Den Vandrette by the harbor.
shopping: Tour the design exhibits at Danish Architecture Center, then head to Hay House.
Pick up ceramics and textiles in the Vesterbro creatives district.
Bike to Jægersborggade for curated vintage and specialty roasters.
sightseeing: Climb the spiral of Church of Our Saviour for a panoramic city sweep.
Float through Nyhavn's pastel harbor with a warm kanelsnegl in hand.
Spend an afternoon at Louisiana Museum of Modern Art by the sea.

description: Table Mountain drama, ocean breezes, and a creative scene stretching from Woodstock to the Winelands.

food: Lunch at The Pot Luck Club overlooking Woodstock's street art.
Trail coffee and pastries at Truth Coffee Roasting, steampunk and all.
Drive to Stellenbosch for a tasting menu at Delaire Graff Estate.
shopping: Browse local makers at the Watershed near the V&A Waterfront.
Score contemporary African design at Oranjezicht City Farm Market.
Hunt for vinyl at Mabu Vinyl in the heart of the city.
sightseeing: Cable car up Table Mountain for sunrise, hike down via Platteklip Gorge.
Road trip Chapman’s Peak Drive hugging dramatic cliffs.
Take the ferry to Robben Island for living history.

description: Color-splashed barrios, progressive cuisine, and museums tucked into every leafy plaza.

food: Reserve a late seating at Pujol for the legendary mole madre course.
Street taco crawl through Condesa and Roma after dark.
Coffee hop between Quentin Café and Cardinal Casa de Café.
shopping: Check out fashion and design pop-ups at LagoAlgo by the lake.
Hunt artisan crafts at La Ciudadela market.
Visit HUSET in Roma Norte for plant-forward home goods and gifts.
sightseeing: Lose yourself in the murals of Palacio de Bellas Artes.
Spend golden hour floating on the canals of Xochimilco.
Day trip to Teotihuacan for sunrise over the pyramids.
